chunk_summary: Therapeutic goals for uncomplicated moderate wasting focus on recovery, prevention of severe wasting, and targeted supplementation based on risk factors, with WHO prioritizing counselling and nutrient-dense home diets.
learning_objectives: Understand the therapeutic goals for managing uncomplicated moderate wasting (MAM).
Identify indications for supplementation and risk-based criteria for its use.
Learn WHO recommendations for nutrient-dense diets and supplementation in MAM.
Recognize the role of counselling and family food access in outpatient care.
key_facts: The primary goal in MAM is recovery from wasting, prevention of SAM, and support for neurodevelopment.
WHO recommends nutrient-dense home diets first, with supplementation for high-risk children (e.g., MUAC 115–119 mm, WAZ < -3, age < 24 months).
In humanitarian contexts, supplementation may be provided to all children aged 6–59 months with moderate wasting.
Lipid-based nutrient supplements (LNS) are conditionally preferred, contributing 40–60% of daily energy needs (100–130 kcal/kg/day).
Supplementation should not displace family foods and must be coupled with a concrete linkage plan for access.
reference_quote: WHO anchors moderate wasting care in a nutrient-dense diet plus identification and treatment of medical and psychosocial drivers.
document_key_topics: Role of counselling and family food access
